for_c_level: This clean run under TLS reduces deployment risks to minimal, confirming production readiness for secure multi-agent memory sharing. Customer-facing claims of seamless AI collaboration are strongly supported with zero failures. No changes from prior runs; patch2 maintains stability.
for_non_technical: AI agents successfully shared and recalled all memories across multiple computers with security enabled, without any mistakes. This means the system allows different AIs to reliably exchange information and work together effectively. Overall, agents do share memories reliably in this setup.
for_sme: All executed scenarios (S1,1b,2,4-6,9-18,22-25,28-42) passed in v0.6.2-patch2 over TLS, with no failure modes in primitives like recall, link, delete, or pubsub. Consistent peer views and replication across nodes indicate robust HTTP/TLS handling. No root causes; run was fully green.
headline: All 35 TLS-secured memory sharing scenarios passed flawlessly.
what_it_proved: Proved consistent, reliable memory replication and retrieval among agents with no data loss or inconsistencies in a secure TLS environment.
what_it_tested: Exercised 35 scenarios testing basic recall, deletion, linking, recovery, hybrid search, pubsub, namespaces, bulk ops, and more across TLS transport in a 4-node HTTP federation mesh.
